response.justification: Aisha presents a strong individual-contributor profile spanning 15+ years with genuine depth in developer tooling — custom test runners (Trial), assertion libraries (Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD infrastructure, and MCP code generators — which partially satisfies the technical focus of the role. However, the position is fundamentally a people management role requiring 2+ years of managing direct reports, and there is zero evidence of this in the resume. Every listed role is an IC position; there is no mention of hiring, conducting 1:1s, setting performance expectations, or owning a team's growth. The technical qualifications are real but insufficient to compensate for the absence of the core management competency.
strengths: Directly relevant developer tooling portfolio: built a custom test runner (Trial), a TDD assertion library (Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD pipelines, and MCP code generators — aligning with the role's focus on testing infrastructure and build systems

Broad polyglot depth across TypeScript, D, C++, Swift, Java, and Node.js with exposure to web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput systems provides the technical foundation needed to guide system design and code review
Community knowledge-sharing through conference talks, open-source projects, and multi-day workshops demonstrates an ability to communicate technical concepts and informally mentor peers
concerns: No people management experience: the resume contains zero evidence of managing direct reports, owning headcount, conducting performance reviews, or building a team — the single most critical requirement for a Level II Engineering Manager
No organizational leadership or change management: the preferred 3 years of leading organizational change is entirely absent, and even informal cross-team technical leadership is not documented
Purely IC career trajectory: every role from 2006 to present is an individual contributor position with no demonstrated or stated interest in transitioning to engineering management

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and testing infrastructure depth
No organizational or team leadership history
